content:
- Completed story-13 task-01 and pushed commit `d7fc41c` on `master`.
- README quick start now explicitly covers bootstrap-script execution, schema export artifact generation, manual grant application before runtime, and an explicit no-repo-internals promise. README verification now lives behind `crates/runner/tests/support/readme_contract.rs` with red-green tests for the novice-path gaps.
- All required lanes passed before completion: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`. The task file is marked `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>`, the plan boxes are checked, and `.ralph/task_switch.sh` already ran.

- Read previous progress log entry and active/done task summaries.
- Confirmed story-13 task-01 was completed previously and current selectable work now depends on remaining failing tasks in stories 13, 14, and 15.
- Reading actual task files next to resolve priority and blocker tags before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- No active failing bug tasks are visible; selection is among unblocked failing non-bug tasks.
- All active remaining tasks currently show <passes>false</passes>; no <blocked_by> tags were present in the active task files inspected so far.
- Checking repo shape (Rust workspace, README, Makefile, Docker/workflow footprint) to ground the next-task choice in the actual implementation state.

- Determined the repo is a Rust 2024 workspace with crates for `runner`, `source-bootstrap`, and `ingest-contract`, plus a real root `Dockerfile` and README-based Docker quick start.
- Evaluated active tasks under the mandatory precedence rules: no unblocked failing bug tasks were active; remaining selectable work was failing non-bug tasks only. No active task file inspected contained a `<blocked_by>` tag or elevated `<priority>` tag.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-13-verify-novice-user/02-task-verify-direct-docker-build-and-run-without-wrapper-scripts.md` and wrote it to `.ralph/current_task.txt` because it is the next logical unblocked verification task in the current novice-user story, directly aligned with the existing Dockerfile, README docker commands, and container test harnesses; story-14 reports and story-15 CI work are downstream.

- Read previous Ralph progress log entries and confirmed the active task had already been selected in story 13.
- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and loaded the `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions from disk as required.
- Reading the active task file next to determine whether this turn is a planning pass or an execution pass.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is still in the pre-execution state with no `<plan>` pointer yet.
- Inspected the story-13 task-01 plan and task file to match Ralph conventions: add a separate `..._plans/...md` file, point to it with `<plan>...</plan>`, and leave execution for a later turn after the plan is marked ready.
- Read the current Docker quick-start README, `crates/runner/tests/readme_contract.rs`, `crates/runner/tests/support/readme_contract.rs`, `crates/runner/tests/long_lane.rs`, and the runner container/image harness support code.
- Key planning finding: the repo already has a real direct `docker build` + `docker run` long lane and a README Docker quick start, but there is no single contract that ties the documented direct commands to executable test support or fails when wrapper scripts creep into the user path.
- Boundary smell for `improve-code-boundaries`: direct container command knowledge is split across README string checks and multiple support helpers (`runner_image_harness.rs`, `runner_container_process.rs`) without one honest owner for the public Docker contract.
- Next immediate step is to write the story-13 task-02 plan, add the `<plan>` path to the task file, mark the plan ready for execution, and quit immediately.

- Created the task-02 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-13-verify-novice-user/02-task-verify-direct-docker-build-and-run-without-wrapper-scripts_plans/2026-04-19-direct-docker-build-run-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` pointer to `.ralph/tasks/story-13-verify-novice-user/02-task-verify-direct-docker-build-and-run-without-wrapper-scripts.md`.
- The plan is marked `NOW EXECUTE` and focuses execution on a typed direct-Docker contract shared across README verification and real long-lane container execution, with `improve-code-boundaries` aimed at removing duplicated Docker command knowledge from scattered test helpers.
- No product code or tests were executed in this turn because the Ralph instructions for the planning state require quitting immediately once the plan is ready.
- Next turn should read the active task and plan, then execute the TDD slices in order: README direct-Docker tracer bullet, no-wrapper contract, CLI alignment, real long-lane direct image path, then full `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.

- Read Ralph progress, current task pointer, and both required skills (`tdd`, `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the active task is story-13 task-02 and prior turn already produced a `NOW EXECUTE` plan.
- Reading the task file and referenced execution plan next so this turn can follow the prescribed TDD slices instead of inventing new scope.

- Loaded the active task file and the referenced plan; it is already in `NOW EXECUTE` state.
- Inspected the README Docker quick start, `readme_contract.rs`, `cli_contract.rs`, `long_lane.rs`, `runner_image_harness.rs`, and `runner_container_process.rs`.
- Confirmed the boundary smell from the plan: direct Docker contract knowledge is split across README prose checks, long-lane literals, and separate image-entrypoint inspection helpers, with no single typed owner.

- Executed TDD slices for the fast contracts:
  - RED/GREEN for a README contract covering direct `docker build` + direct `docker run`.
  - RED/GREEN for explicit no-wrapper-script rejection in the Docker quick start.
  - RED/GREEN for a CLI help contract that derives the Docker-documented subcommand inventory from one shared support owner.
- Extracted `crates/runner/tests/support/runner_docker_contract.rs` as the direct-Docker contract owner and began reusing it from the long-lane harness and container-process support to flatten duplicated command assembly.
- Next step is to finish verifying the executable Docker slice by running the ignored long-lane test, then move on to the full repository lanes.

- Repository-wide lanes reached `make test-long` and exposed a shared regression outside the new direct-image long lane: six `default_bootstrap_long_lane` scenarios now fail with `destination customers did not converge ... actual=<empty>` at `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs:293`.
- The failing set is consistent with the container-mode E2E runner path used by the default bootstrap harnesses, which points back to the recent refactor in `runner_container_process.rs` rather than the new direct-image `long_lane.rs` path.
- Reading the harness around the failing assertion and rerunning one representative ignored long-lane test in isolation next to confirm the exact command-shape regression before patching it.

- Diagnosed the `make test-long` regression to the new shared `docker run` builder: it hard-coded `127.0.0.1:{port}:8443`, which broke container-to-host access for the container-mode E2E harnesses that send webhooks through `host.docker.internal`.
- Fixed the contract by making the host bind IP explicit in `runner_docker_contract.rs`, keeping `Some("127.0.0.1")` for the direct-image smoke lane and restoring the old unrestricted published-port shape (`None`) for `runner_container_process.rs`.
- Verified the representative failing long lane `ignored_long_lane_bootstraps_a_default_cockroach_source_into_real_postgres_tables` now passes in isolation; rerunning the required repository gates from the final state next.

- Full `make test-long` narrowed remaining breakage to one recovery lane: `ignored_long_lane_recovers_after_reconcile_transaction_failure` failed because the shared Docker runtime builder added `--rm`, so the failed runner container vanished before `wait_for_failed_exit()` could inspect it.
- Updated the typed launch spec to carry `auto_remove` explicitly: the direct-image harness keeps `auto_remove: true`, while the container-mode E2E harness restores `auto_remove: false` so recovery tests can inspect failed containers.
- Verifying the fix with `make check` plus the isolated failing recovery long lane before re-running the full long-lane sweep.

- Completed the direct-Docker contract task end to end.
- Final green state from the current tree: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed after two follow-up fixes in the shared Docker runtime launch contract (host bind IP and auto-remove semantics) that restored existing E2E behavior while keeping the new direct-image contract owner.
- Updated the task file to `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>` and am running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then will commit and push all changes including `.ralph` artifacts.
